steps.title: Identify what is being compared
steps.content: The opener "Compared with that of a highly concentrated market" uses "that" as a stand-in for "HHI score." So one side of the comparison is the HHI score of a highly concentrated market. The blank must begin with the matching item: the HHI score of a less concentrated market.

steps.title: Require parallel structure
steps.content: A comparison phrase like "Compared with X, Y..." needs Y to be the same kind of thing as X. Here X = an HHI score, so Y must also be an HHI score. The subject right after the comma must be the HHI score, not the market itself.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'a market that is less concentrated will have a much lower HHI score': subject is "a market," not "an HHI score" — breaks parallelism (compares a score to a market). ✗
  • Choice B — 'the HHI score of a less concentrated market will be much lower': subject is "the HHI score," mirroring the score on the other side of the comparison. ✓
  • Choice C — 'when a market is less concentrated, its HHI score will be much lower': turns the second side into a conditional clause whose subject is "a market," not a score — breaks parallelism. ✗
  • Choice D — 'a less concentrated market will have an HHI score that is much lower': subject is "a less concentrated market," not a score — breaks parallelism. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
